prompt:
Two asks for the breadcrumb builder (breadcrumbs.ts) before the EU launch.

Localized labels: buildBreadcrumbs grows options.locale plus options.localeLabels — a locale-keyed map of the same href->label shape we already use. Resolution per crumb: exact locale, then its language part ('de-CH' -> 'de'), then the existing default labels map, then the humanizer, same as now. The home crumb localizes through a '/' entry; failing that, homeLabel/'Home' as today.

Collapse: deep pages give us absurd trails, design wants a middle-collapse. options.maxItems (must be >= 3, else RangeError — an ellipsis needs home and a tail around it). When the trail is longer than maxItems, emit exactly maxItems crumbs: home first, then ONE ellipsis crumb, then the last maxItems-2 real crumbs. The ellipsis crumb is { label: '…', href: '', current: false, ellipsis: true, collapsed: [...] } where collapsed holds the hidden crumbs, in order, fully labelled (locale rules apply — design renders them in a dropdown). At or under the limit: output identical to today, and I mean identical — no stray ellipsis/collapsed properties on ordinary crumbs, our snapshot tests deepEqual these objects.

Acceptance suite is test_locale_collapse.ts; test_breadcrumbs.ts is current behavior and must stay green. node --test test_breadcrumbs.ts test_locale_collapse.ts, and the test files are hands-off.
